book_claims.claim: Whole-grain bread used in the Longevity Diet as a breakfast and side item.

supplementary_research.finding: Whole-wheat bread significantly reduces postprandial glucose and insulin responses compared to refined wheat; associated with lower risk of type 2 diabetes.
Whole grain consumption (≥3 servings/day) associated with 22% lower all-cause mortality in meta-analysis of prospective cohort studies.

culinary_pairings.tradition: Ligurian focaccia dipped in extra-virgin olive oil

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Fermentable fiber feeds butyrate-producing microbiota; slows glucose absorption
Bound form released by gut microbiota; antioxidant activity against lipid peroxidation
Retained in whole grain; support energy metabolism and DNA methylation

synergies.description: Oleic acid slows gastric emptying, lowering glycemic impact; polyphenols add anti-inflammatory effects
Rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid in rosemary complement ferulic acid in whole wheat for combined antioxidant effects
Pairing with legumes creates complete amino acid profile and combined fiber types
